reviews.review_msg: Sara is a good teacher. She gives clear lectures but quite difficult quizzes. Also for the midterm, beware of the time limit.
The best prof to take MACM101 with.

He is genuinely very kind and goes out of his way to help. All you gotta do is reach out to him.
The course itself is kind of hard but his exams are very similar to the assignments and the tutorial questions. So make sure to practice similar questions, and you do not need to stress over MACM if you're in his class!!
Also, the textbook is free.
Absolute legend, takes time to fully explain his lectures and his midterm and final are very reasonable. Made MACM101 easy and very doable. 100% recommended, best prof for MACM101.

One of the best profs at SFU! Super clear, supportive, and actually cares about his students' success. Take him if you can!
True Goat!

W prof, course was 5 assignments worth 20%, attendance tutorial for 10% (they only did it for the first half of the course), 30% on midterm 1 on first 2 chapters, 40% on final on everything after (non cumulative.) Midterm and final were manageable if you did tutorials, plus he added a 10 point scale to our raw grades which bumped me up quite a bit

One of the best professor for MACM101. His lectures are easy to follow if you pay attention, and the slides covers everything you need to know to do the assignments. The exams are based off of the tutorial questions so as long as you understand the slides and do the tutorial questions, then it should be a fairly straightforward course.
Slides are easy to read and exams are fair to what is taught in class/tutorials

This professor may be the best professor for MACM101. His lectures are easy to follow if you pay attention. His slides are well done and the tests directly reflects the slides. The assignments were challenging but nothing to be stressed out about. 

Tip for students: Study tutorial and slide questions! Or you risk failing.

I would not recommend. Lectures seem unconnected to what he tests. Assignments are quite different from tests. Incredibly boring lectures. The textbook assigned was old and boring. I ended up just self studying the Rosen Discrete math textbook. 1 out of maybe 10 lectures he will say something useful.

His notes were disorganized and made no sense. Avoid this professor at all times. I got advice from others that he was a prof to avoid, and I thought if I worked hard in this course, I could still do it. Oh i was so wrong. It doesn't matter how much effort you put into this course. His exams and assignments had no resemblance to notes whatsoever.

His lectures are monotone, the notes he writes are useless after looking back on them a few days later. His choice in examples used in lectures are often complicated, and he would often scratch out things after second guessing himself (he changed his solution 6 times once in class). MACM is already a difficult subject, even tougher with him.

worst teacher ever. got good marks in assignment and close to average in midterm. but failed final exam but few marks which gave me D. However, the class is curved. If i calculate my marks, I should get at least C- but this guy gave me D(after curved). Does not revealed how he curved it. He cannot even teach and youtube will save you.

All the lecture notes were hand written and only formula without any explanation. Only thing he did in class was just reading that hand written notes that he wrote previously without any explanation when to use this formula. All the exam questions were not even close to the textbook practice question. Maybe cause i am CMNS major

(Fall 2023)

Tbh: avoid. Dry lectures with no recorded lectures for review, and no pre-posted slides. You'll need to rely on the textbook and the scanned lect. notes. Def read ahead in the textbook before lectures and hope you get a good TA to help explain things. Midterm and final averages were both low 50s, so take that as you will. Good luck!

MACM ON STEROIDS FOR NO REASON
Reasons why you should avoid this prof at all costs:
1. Be ready to self teach. f* his lectures of hand written notes and stick to the textbook
2. You will be confused. The assignments introduce concepts that are taught later down the road and some are not based on the lectures
3. The curve will be your only hope GLHF

This was perhaps the most difficult class I have taken in 5 years of university. Expect to fully read the textbook plus watch tons of online courses on the material. Exams were very difficult. I personally really liked Dr Tofiloski and think he made the most of a very tricky subject. Part marks given if you went to him and explained your solution.

Lectures can be disorganized, but Milan is flexible with deadlines. Textbook is unclear, so use Unstuck study and other resources to supplement. Overall, a challenging but doable course with a generous curve.

Cancels lectures randomly and does the bare minimum in lectures. If you don't mind teaching yourself he's alright because the midterms weren't horrible and the curve was alright, but as a professor he is pretty bad.

Horrible at teaching as everyone else has said. You are better off watching Youtube videos than going to his lectures. He cancels classes last minute which apparently is a common thing for him to do in other classes he teaches as well.

Classes got cancelled randomly, the homework felt nothing like what we learned in lecture, and leads to just using Youtube videos to learn the concepts. Tested on things that he had never fully covered in class, and marking was inconsistent. Though, the curve was generous and he did seem to want to prevent people from failing.
Dont takeit

Disorganized. Classes got cancelled on short notice. Homework took ages to be graded. Unhelpful exam outlines. The textbook is almost as unclear as Milan, be ready to teach yourself. Lectures are a drag, examples are just copied from the textbook. Milan somehow manages to come across as even more unenthusiastic about this course than the students.
I got curve but due to his messy nature course was a hassle

As the other reviews say Milan is definitely not the most organized nor a great teacher. However, his flexibility with deadlines throughout the course as well as a relatively free final made up for it. Not to mention the curve was pretty generous!
L PROF
Professor lacks basic teaching skills, fails to return assignments, and ignores emails. Zero engagement, disappointing experience.
This class easily bombed my GPA because of his midterm2 outline being so vague, and then using the hardest q's from a chapter he didnt teach. Thanks Milan!

If you have the chance to take the course w a diff prof, do so. We were ghosted the entire sem, MTs were delayed, classes constantly cancelled, and as a result our final mark is delayed. Someone who lacks this much should not be a professor. AVOID.

this prof is so incompetent! our hw didnt get marked for months on end, mt2 outline was disgustingly vague (and resulted in a 40avg), and now he rushed hw marks and doesn't have our final grade back despite td being the grade deadline. to future takers, PLEASE AVOID. he cant teach, and he read off tb the entire sem. he also doesnt know d/dx x^2. :P

this "instructor" shouldn't be allowed to teach at SFU. assignments were assigned and *NO* feedback was given whatsoever, as well as releasing all the homework grades on the last day.. His lectures are monotone including his "lecture videos" and he CANNOT explain any topic at any level. He is inaccessible outside of class, and ghosts emails. AVOID

This professor shouldn't even be a professor. His lecture slides are copied/pasted from the textbook. You will never get anything out of the lecture. His exams are also a curveball. He has made plenty of mistakes on his exams, and due dates always are pushed back at a very late notice. Very unorganized and unprofessional. Avoid at all costs.
Literally the worst professor, you are better off watching youtube instead of his lectures

Boring, unclear, makes plenty of mistakes in his lecture notes, messy lecture notes.
 AVOID!!!!!!!

He is the worst of the worst! Avoid him by all means. His lectures make you more confused. His writing is messy and he often makes mistakes. It's better to read the textbook on your own. He is very unorganized and unclear. There's no homework for 1 month and 2 homework in 6 days. He should be fired.
His lectures are useless
one of the weirdest teacher i've known, maybe he's smart but he doesnt know how to teach...His lectures are all over the place, I dont konw what he's talking about....bottom line, sucks
Worse professor I've ever had in my entire life.  He definitely knows his stuffs, but does not know how to teach...at all.

Bobby is a really enthusiastic teacher and his YouTube videos are readily accessible, but his MACM material is quite tough and difficult to deal with; it doesn't help that some of his decisions are questionable, like doing a lecture of new material right before a midterm.
Simple assignment and exams, you may not need to spend lots of time on this course. Remeber to take notes, read textbook and do homework.
Reads 100 slides within an hour which is too fast for most students . Does not stop and discourages questions. Do not take unless you have no other option as a professor.
Just Awful.

Pro:
exams didn't have mistakes.
Gives clear info on what on the exam.
Will replace midterm grades with final's if you get higher marks on final.
Con:
Lectures go through materials quickly.
Unlikely to answer questions during lecture.
Lecture notes are reused from previous years.

You won't get much from his teaching style as he just reads off slides. First midterm screwed me over since I did practice questions from the textbook but most of the midterm were different types of questions straight from the slides. You'll have better luck predicting what questions on the slides will be on the exams than actually studying

The lectures were not helpful at all. He read off the slides and skipped multiple of them, claiming that they were so easy that he didn't need to teach them to us. He would not explain anything and was not willing to answer questions. If you take this course, you will have to teach yourself everything.

Lectures almost useless as he neither knows how nor cares to make them actually educational. Tutorials are only Q&A. If you are new to math and need guidance, then you'll get none. You have to learn everything on your own from the slides. Otherwise the course itself is doable. 2/5 since at least he isn't actively making the course impossible

unless you pay him off you're not getting any kind of good grade in this class bro intentionally mislead us for every test after we scored too high on the first midterm and we ended up with the lowest class average of all time. big homie is losing it

Steve seems to know the topic well, however he doesn't know how to teach. Whenever someone asked a question, he would make remarks about how dumb the question was, or even make fun of the student. Whenever he was teaching something, he would make remarks about how it was so easy he could do it in his sleep, and so skip over half the explanation.

HORRIBLE dude if you have him pray that you can pass his class. He spends the entire lecture nonsensically reading the slides but getting confused about his own material. Would cancel class randomly after we were already there because he had a headache. did this like 6 times in a row right before our midterm. how this man is still a professor here
you're either gonna love or hate pearce. very interesting person

He can't teach, very hard to understand his lectures (don't expect to) but his slides are good and majority of exams is straight from the powerpoints. Go to lectures even if you don't understand a thing because he gives away which questions are on the exams. Not a good experience at all but he's a man of his word and bought the whole class pizza
Very rough class. He is a pretty funny guy, but he cannot teach. Would not reccomend taking his classes, but if you need to do so you should get ready for a lot of self studying.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS IT'S NOT WORTH IT. 

Completely self-taught course, only merit to going to lecture is getting exam questions that he loosely gives out randomly
Only went to his lectures to have fun watching him harass students, bro cannot teach. Do not take this class💀
He's really not that bad, he's very uhhh interesting, so I'm never bored in class. He's also a man of his word, he said he would buy pizza for us and buy us pizza he did.
How this man is still employed I do not know. He is an inspiration to us all that you can get paid to not know how to do your job.

Took in the summer sem. Lectures are very content heavy, but engaging so be ready to learn on your own. Attending lecture is absolutely necessary as he often gives away exam questions. His exams are copy paste questions from the lectures, so as long as you study those, the exams are extremely easy. He is a funny guy, just pay attention in lectures.

Avoid at all costs, doesn't have any contact info for both himself and the TA's in the class. Doesn't respond to the discussions channel on Canvas and doesn't explain absolutely any incorrections on assignments and midterms/final. Always bullies and ridicules students in class and plays it off as a joke. Never taking anything with him again.
He has a good sense of humor. Do not skip the lectures and you'll be fine. Best prof!
Best class ever, this was so entertaining, amazing professor, I had 60% average and got a B+

Steven Pearce gets a bad rap for his crazy antics but he doesn't make the class difficult. He literally told us exactly what would be on the midterms and finals, as in the exact questions, which you could then memorize. So if you are after a good grade I would take this prof. He is also pretty funny in class, and mostly chill. You wont be bored.

Very knowledgeable and smart prof, so it's worth to attend and listen to him. The biggest problem of this prof is you won't be able to get complete answers for your assignments. He shows solutions for SOME questions but not all. So, you don't know if your answers are correct for the other questions (TA marks the questions the prof showed answers).

Hard to understand but not too too bad. Used another teacher's Powerpoint notes for every lecture, expanded upon them, but not in any really enlightening ways.  Constantly made mistakes. Expect to learn everything on your own. I'd really really recommend studying in advance of the course. 25% of last year's students got D or lower. 27% got C- to C+
Great professor, organized notes

His lecture style is him doing problems on paper which at first looks like dread but it will help you a lot and he gives you a very, very good and generous outline of what's directly on the exams + final exam. He cares about your learning, very good professor to take MACM with!
Lectures are pretty good and he gives you a pretty good idea of what will be on the exam. TAs aren't great but overall a solid prof

Not sure why there is so much excessive praise for Kay. Although he is clear with expectations both in terms of the lecture delivery and what he wants from students, the exams and the marking that he uses to assess student understanding of the course material are far from fair and reasonable.

Kay is a good professor, but falls short in a few areas. His writing can be hard to read at times, which can slow the pace of the lecture when you're trying to make sense of what he's writing down. Lectures follow the textbooks too closely, which de-values the lectures since you could skip and study solo. Thankfully, his exams are pretty fair.

MACM 101 is a tough course for most people but Kay taught the material in a very digestible way. He's a generous grader and his exams are extremely reasonable - he even told us the exact questions that would be on the final. Do the work, don't fall behind, and you'll do just fine

Wiese clearly cares about the success of his students. He is extremely reasonable in his assignment and test expectations and he is very organized and thorough with his lectures. Trust me when I say if you have to take MACM 101, take it with him.

This guy has been by far my favorite prof in my first term here by a longshot. His lectures are well structured, his notes are detailed with good examples and he's overall just a good dude. Final is worth 50% but he tells you what kind of questions to expect and prep for. If you need MACM please try to take it with him, even if its a 3 hour lecs.
GOAT for computer science students, both CMPT and MACM courses. Unlike many he actually wants his students to achieve high grades.

Best prof for macm 101 at sfu.
Absolute legend.
GOAT that's all I have to say

He just doesnt care about class, hes just reading slides in lecture. You learn nothing. Exams are simple, you just have to memorize all the theorems and you would pass but there is no partial marks at all.

Horrible time management. Doesnt care much about the class. Heavy accent. Be ready to do alot of self learning. There r 15 mins YouTube video that taught the lectures better than Andrei's 70 mins video. Hws are VERY difficult. U will need external sources for help. He made it VERY clear about his expectations, & that ur problems r urs only, not his

I am not a huge math person, but Prof. Abulatov made me really enjoy a math class. He was a little intimidating in person when getting help, however, he really made me understand the material during lecture. If you get a chance take his class!

Lectures are very engaging and he explains all the concepts well. Lots of practice from weekly quizzes (not for grades) and assignments that cover most stuff. Midterms and exams will be a breeze if you do the practice, and the sample midterm he provides. Cons: Rushes near the end of lectures due to time and always goes past end time

MACM 101 with Dr. Ternovska was a good experience. We got feedback on assignments *before* resubmission for grading which guaranteed high marks. Furthermore, the example midterm/finals were very reflective of the actual exams. She's clearly passionate about sharing the fun & discovery of math with us. Office hours were great too, don't be shy!

It was clear she was asked last minute to teach this class as she was using the other teacher's slides to teach. She did not provide good material to prepare for the midterms however after the second midterm's average was low, she made the final easier to compensate.

Go with other proffesors, or you would:
- not understand anything in class and would be suffering in every single lecture
- Chatgpt and TrevTutor and notebook and YOU would be your only teachers
- She is CARELESS, DOESNT know how to TEACH so yeah why should study with someone who doesnt actually know how to do their job, like literally!

I didn't understand on her in the lectures, I used YouTube videos to understand the topics. The course is not well organized. Even the tutorials were not that beneficial, the TA doesn't really know the answer for most of students' questions
Cant explain properly in class and seems confused herself. Her speech isnt fluent and she doesnt speak confidently. Doesnt give fluent explanations.
honestly the worst teacher I've had, if you think you are smart enough to pass you aren't think again. she seems like a genuine lady but just not a teacher by any means.

Be ready for the greatest sleep of your life during his lectures. It's like listening to a quantum mechanics documentary but you are a dance student. So boring and no engagement. I slept through most of my lectures in the first half of the semester, and didn't attend at all for the rest. Trev Tutor on youtube is your best friend. Class's curved. W
Okay but homework sometimes gets really hard.

I really enjoyed his classes! He provides skeleton lecture notes & lecture recordings that expire. His lectures were filled with lots of examples, and he provided proofs of important theorems. His explanations were very clear and well-paced.The one really difficult part was graph theory, which was recently added to the course material.

Just don't, people were literally crying in the office hours before the final. Its a lost cause. Macm101 is already a tuff class for first years, a totally new concept we have never seen before, and he is so rude and arrogant, the homework is insanely hard let alone the exams, lecture only go on the definition of things not how to actually do them
With him, it's even harder than MACM 201 or MACM 316. Just a heads-up for beginners who don't want to end up questioning their abilities or feeling like they can't do it

I would never recommend this guy. He mocked a student for not knowing a specific card game, then said he'd better learn it if he wants to pass the course. Unprofessional and condescending. He's a smart guy but beyond that, the class environment wasn't supportive or respectful AT ALL. Proceed EXTREMEEEE with caution.

Extremely hard professor, will give you mad imposter syndrome, nervous breakdowns and will make you want to switch your major; but if you survive you'll have mad skills in the subject. I say take his course but be prepared for a nightmarishly hard time.
Tough class, but the professor is entertaining. Lectures can be confusing, so lean on the textbook, tutorials, and unstuckstudy AI for extra help and practice.
bad bart

Had no regard for students, stopped class for 20 minutes one time to flame a student who took a picture of his slides for a 50 minute class. Shut you down for not knowing something. Somehow taught a course without teaching. Class is basically determined by the final exam regardless of how you did on the HW/MT. AVOID AVOID.

Took this class twice, failed the first time. During my 2nd time taking this course, when I asked to review my final from the last semester he told me no, yet another student was allowed to review his final from the previous term.
sad fart

very funny and good at teaching its just a really hard class, midterms and final were pretty hard but there are lots of office hours and tutorials were helpful as well. lectures were sometimes hard to follow along but if you ask questions he will answer

ruthless prof. it's like he wants you to fail. no lecture recordings, no completed notes for you to review, lectures delivered at a speedrun pace, and you can't even take pictures of the slides. if you don't have a strong background in math, only take if you are willing to spend disproportionately more time on this course than your other courses.

Went over theorms but never gave exmaples on how to use them. the assignments extremly hard to do also. was not present for two weeks in the semester. And canceled his office hours in the finals week.overall not a great experience

This is someone whom you would call a con artist. His lectures are all over the place and include rants that make you lose focus. He gives you questions on assignments that he hardly teaches you how to solve them. He only shows you the definitions and theorems, but no strategy on how to use them towards any of his assignments or tests

Stay away if you have no prior knowledge and want to be happy!! He seems so unapproachable and can be quite harsh and rude. He teaches in a way where he expects to know already know what he's talking about. The assignments are so difficult and not very similar to the lectures.

Has a penchant for obscure verbal meanderings when it comes to his teaching style, since he introduces the material in a way as if you already learned it during his lectures. Enjoys hooking answers from students through in-class votes for his own amusement instead of desconstructing the material in a way that freshmen CS students can grasp.

Brad can be unnecessarily harsh when dealing with students. I found his lectures quite disengaging and not helpful at all. MACM as a course is inherently difficult but I feel like Brads attitude doesnt help. This class is VERY test heavy with 80% of your grade going towards tests (16%x2 midterms + 48% finals). I beg of you take it with Bulatov.

Brad teaches the course as if you've already learned the material. Handwriting is barely legible. Brad does not post finished notes. He is unapproachable and unpleasant to deal with. He makes a big deal out of small things i.e. stops the class and rants for 2 minutes when someone's phone goes off. Don't expect him to help you if you're struggling.

He writes fast and messy so his notes are almost impossible to copy down, I have not left a single lecture with complete notes. Photos of filled slides are not allowed, he does not post them either. He is quite rude and arrogant when dealing with students refusing to accommodate for circumstances outside of students' control.
Brad Bart definitely cares for education but goes through slides fast, pretty rude and doesn't answer questions. Definitely the hardest prof of all MACM101 profs. GG

Ramesh's teachings are mediocre at best; Class moves slow and is boring. Taking MACM 101 with another prof will almost guarantee a better learning outcome/experience. Ramesh is also almost never accessible out side of class. Midterms takes a month to mark and another two months to see your exam. Overall, TERRIBLE prof.
lecture slides are useless and ramesh can't teach at all. He just reads off the lecture slides with no emotion.

His lecture materials( class slides ) are arranged in a unreadable way and also, there are several concept mistakes in his given slides. Really frustrating when trying to apply these wrong theorems. Besides this, he only reads his slides out loud during lecture, not caring if people is keeping up.

the course isnt necessarily hard and ramesh isnt even necessarily a bad prof if you go to his office hours and ask for help... its just that his lectures in class are literally so bland and boring because he reads his notes out loud with little to no extra input... you can still do well in the class with self studying and trevtutor... lol
During the lectures he usually reads through his slides with few explanation, frustrating.
Probably the best MACM 101 professor out there. Practice the textbook questions a lot and really understand the assignment quizzes. They help a ton.
Amazing teacher for macm. Amazing lectures, he made everything so simple and easy to understand. Definitely take him for macm 101
if you have the option take him

This is his first time teaching. First impression wise, it can get a bit awkward from time to time. However, his lecture slide is very useful and clear. His exams are fair. He is also very responsive either through email or meeting him in person. Pay attention to his lecture slides. Some questions may come up during the exam.

One of the best professors I've met at SFU, do not hesitate to take his class. Aside from well-taught lectures, he makes really good jokes. I honestly wished he taught more courses, especially the CMPT ones.
His exams are fair enough. Make sure you study lecture slides and sample questions given by the prof!

Ryan is overall an amazing instructor! The course material is famous for being hard, but he did a very good job of teaching. His exams are fair, grading from TAs can sometimes be weird, but if you bring it up to him, he will fix it for you Tips for future students: ALWAYS ALWAYS ALWAYS review his slides, he will take questions from there on exams.
